title: Most Edge Functions still log full Supabase error objects to Supabase's structured logs
evidence: e.g. update_player/index.ts:94 `console.error('update_player: fetch error', fetchError)` and join_room/index.ts:66 `console.error('join_room: fetch error', fetchError)` pass the full Supabase/Postgrest error object (message, Postgres code, details, hint, status) into console.error. Two call sites already follow the safer pattern — submit_action/index.ts:39 `console.error('submit_action: rpc error', error?.message ?? error)` and _shared/mod.ts:95 which logs `error.message` — showing the fix pattern is known but was only partially rolled out (a prior lens pass on 2026-06-25 flagged this same gap across all 10 functions; it is unresolved in ~9 of the ~13 remaining call sites).
impact: Postgres internal schema details (column/constraint names, query fragments) land in Supabase Edge Function logs, visible to anyone with dashboard/log access. No service-role key or secret is exposed, but it aids schema enumeration and is inconsistent with the safer pattern already adopted in two call sites.
recommendation: Standardize all console.error(...) calls in supabase/functions/*/index.ts to log `error?.message ?? error?.code ?? error` instead of the raw error object, matching the pattern already used in submit_action/index.ts:39 and _shared/mod.ts:95.
acceptance_criteria: All console.error calls across supabase/functions/*/index.ts log error.message/error.code rather than the full error object
